# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that deploys directly to production when code is pushed to the main branch. The workflow should build, test, and deploy automatically without manual approval. This enables our continuous deployment workflow for rapid iteration.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD to Production

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production # Designates this job as deploying to the 'production' environment

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js (example for a JavaScript project)
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20' # Specify your project's Node.js version

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci # Or yarn install, pnpm install, etc.

      - name: Build project
        run: npm run build # Or yarn build, pnpm build, etc.

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test # Or yarn test, pnpm test, etc.

      - name: Deploy to Production
        # Replace this with your actual deployment commands.
        # This could involve:
        # - Uploading files to a cloud storage (e.g., AWS S3, Azure Blob Storage, GCP Cloud Storage)
        # - Deploying to a PaaS (e.g., Heroku, Vercel, Netlify, Azure App Service, AWS Elastic Beanstalk)
        # - Building and pushing Docker images to a container registry
        # - SSHing into a server and running deployment scripts
        run: |
          echo "Starting deployment to production environment..."
          # Example: Deploying static files to an S3 bucket
          # aws s3 sync ./build s3://your-production-bucket --delete
          # Example: Deploying a Docker image
          # docker build -t your-app:latest .
          # docker push your-registry/your-app:latest
          # kubectl apply -f kubernetes/deployment.yaml
          echo "Deployment to production completed successfully!"
